topic: Drift census over packet 036-deep-loop-innovation: revalidate all 15 planned implementation phases 003-017 against commits after baseline 0ce43ff589, separating first-order path drift from second-order premise drift and resolving the required controls and dependencies.

note: Fan-out lineage override is authoritative; spec anchoring, memory save, staging, and any reducer path resolving outside this lineage are skipped.

focus: Independently reproduce phase 003 runtime-reference and behavior-benchmark path drift, then resolve the packet-033 renumber dependency.
noveltyJustification: Independently confirmed three positive controls and resolved the renamed benchmark packet into distinct historical-provenance and active-execution locations.
keyQuestions: Which paths, files, globs, symbols, and dependencies named by phases 003-017 no longer resolve?
Which phase premises are false because mode counts, routing defaults, taxonomy, or capabilities changed?
Does the packet-033 benchmark dependency survive its renumber, and what exact location should phases 003 and 016 use?
What is the verdict for phases 003-010?
What is the verdict for phases 011-017, including a clean negative control?
answeredQuestions: Phase 003's two runtime-reference paths were renamed to kebab-case by cc77a1e550a.
Phase 003's behavior_benchmark glob has zero matches and must use behavior-benchmark.
Packet 033 survives as z_archive/027 after 7f3216fc5024; active execution uses system-deep-loop shared and per-mode behavior-benchmark paths.
Phase 003 provisionally needs refinement rather than invalidation.
ruledOut: Packet-033 benchmark content was deleted.
Phase 016 should execute a runner from the archived packet.

focus: Run a first-order path/file/symbol/glob resolution census over phases 004-017 and identify a genuinely clean negative-control phase.
noveltyJustification: Classified first-order resolution across phases 004-017, established phase 004 as a renumber-safe clean negative control, and separated a malformed pre-baseline phase-006 reference from post-baseline drift.
keyQuestions: Which paths, files, globs, symbols, and dependencies named by phases 003-017 no longer resolve, including phase 003's required positive controls?
Which phase premises are now false because registered-mode counts, routing defaults, taxonomy, or capabilities changed or already shipped?
Does the packet-033 benchmark dependency survive its renumber, and what exact benchmark location should phases 003 and 016 use now?
What is the explicit verdict for each phase 003-010, with commit SHA and path:line evidence?
What is the explicit verdict for each phase 011-017, including at least one genuinely clean negative control, with commit SHA and path:line evidence?
answeredQuestions: Phase 004 is a genuinely clean first-order negative control: all parent, manifest, and child targets resolve after post-baseline packet renumbers 7f3216fc5024 and 8d3b5b21d57.
No genuinely missing current target was found for phases 007-017 after packet-root shorthand and planned future artifacts were normalized.
Phase 006's malformed ../../002 research reference is a baseline defect rather than post-baseline drift.
Phase 005's named source, test, prototype, and buildLineageCommand surfaces still resolve after e4b242c3940c.
ruledOut: Treating packet-root shorthand in phases 012 and 015-017 as phase-directory-relative missing files.
Treating phase 005's abbreviated runtime test paths as missing.
Counting phase 006's malformed research reference as post-baseline drift.
Counting planned execution outputs or newly authored child contracts as targets that disappeared after baseline.

current system-deep-loop runtime source and unit tests

focus: Resolve phase 013's registered-mode/workstream taxonomy and test whether phase 005's exact top-level live-web-search propagation capability already shipped.
noveltyJustification: Separated seven public modes, five implementation families, three improvement variants, and eight phase-013 workstreams; proved the named routing commits did not change mode count; and established that phase 005's exact cli-codex web-search contract remains unshipped despite partial fan-out infrastructure.
keyQuestions: Which phase premises are now false because registered-mode counts, routing defaults, taxonomy, or planned capabilities changed or already shipped?
What is the explicit verdict for each phase 003-010, with commit SHA and path:line evidence?
What is the explicit verdict for each phase 011-017, including at least one genuinely clean negative control, with commit SHA and path:line evidence?
answeredQuestions: Phase 013 has seven public registered modes, five implementation workflow families, three improvement variants, and eight migration workstreams; deep-improvement-common is the extra non-public workstream.
The seven-mode count is unchanged from baseline 0ce43ff589; phase 013's eight-modes wording is a baseline taxonomy defect and the phase needs refinement.
Commits 6cd8ab14e4e, 708d25acf04, and 908efde8d8f changed activation, typed leaf routing, and the zero-signal default, not registered-mode count.
Phase 005's exact liveTools.webSearch and cli-codex --search exec capability has not shipped; e4b242c3940 is partial cli-opencode support only, so phase 005 remains still valid.
ruledOut: Counting deep-improvement-common as a public registered mode.
Treating runtimeLoopType values as the public mode inventory.
Inferring mode-count drift from typed leaf-resource routing.
Treating e4b242c3940's cli-opencode launch fix as phase 005 completion.

focus: Produce a complete provisional 15-row verdict matrix for phases 003-017 by reconciling iterations 1-3 with each phase's current purpose.
noveltyJustification: Produced the first complete phase-by-phase verdict surface, preserved phase 004 as the clean control, separated three refinement cases from non-invalidated phases, and isolated a bounded second-order gap for every provisional row.
keyQuestions: Which paths, files, globs, symbols, and dependencies named by phases 003-017 no longer resolve?
Which phase premises remain unsupported after first-order drift is separated from second-order drift?
What is the explicit verdict for each phase 003-010?
What is the explicit verdict for each phase 011-017?
answeredQuestions: All phases 003-017 now have an explicit provisional verdict with commit and current path:line evidence.
Phase 004 remains the clean negative control.
No phase meets the invalidation bar; phases 003, 006, and 013 need refinement.
Each low-confidence row now names its smallest unresolved second-order evidence gap.
questionsRemaining: Which phase-006 through phase-012 capabilities already shipped partially or completely?
Have phase-014 authority semantics or phase-015 retirements already landed?
Does phase 016 require only benchmark-path and taxonomy refinement?
ruledOut: Promoting first-order path survival to proof of premise validity.
Invalidating phase 013 solely because eight workstreams were mislabeled as eight public modes.
Counting phase 006's pre-baseline malformed link as post-baseline drift.
Treating planned child artifacts and gate outputs as deleted dependencies.

focus: Inspect the current system-deep-loop runtime and map shipped event/ledger, evidence/control, compatibility, orchestration, intelligence, convergence, and shared-contract capabilities onto phases 006-012.
noveltyJustification: Converted seven provisional phase rows into full acceptance-contract comparisons, identified substantial reusable substrate, and changed phases 007, 010, 011, and 012 to needs refinement without finding any core-purpose-complete phase.
keyQuestions: Which phase-006 through phase-012 capabilities have already shipped partially or completely in the current runtime?
Does shipped overlap alter a phase's starting assumptions or satisfy its full core purpose?
Which provisional verdicts change after separating reusable substrate from complete acceptance contracts?
answeredQuestions: Every phase 006-012 now has a capability-overlap classification with introducing commit and current path:line evidence.
No phase 006-012 is invalidated because no current capability satisfies its full core purpose.
Phases 007, 010, 011, and 012 need refinement because current substrate changes their scope or premise.
Phases 008 and 009 remain still valid, while phase 006 remains needs refinement.

Does phase 016 require only benchmark-path and taxonomy refinement, or broader gate changes?
ruledOut: Inferring phase completion from capability-bearing filenames.
Invalidating phase 011 because generic convergence substrate exists.

focus: Inspect current runtime authority selectors/state migration, legacy writers and archival readers, whole-system gate/benchmark inventory, and phase 017's moving-HEAD premise to finalize phases 014-017.
noveltyJustification: Finalized phases 014, 015, and 017 as still valid and changed phase 016 to needs refinement after proving that five benchmark packages and component checks do not constitute the required exact-SHA eight-workstream whole-system gate.
keyQuestions: Have phase-014 authority semantics or phase-015 legacy-writer retirements already landed?
Does phase 016 require only benchmark-path and taxonomy refinement, or broader gate changes?
Does phase 017's integrate-latest premise remain valid against current HEAD?
answeredQuestions: No canonical per-mode authority selector, epoch, cutover certificate, or rollback-window contract has shipped, so phase 014 remains still valid.
Active writers and compatibility readers remain but no zero-use, closed-window, fail-closed retirement gate has shipped, so phase 015 remains still valid.
Phase 016 needs broader refinement because five benchmark packages and current component checks do not compose the exact-SHA eight-workstream acceptance gate.
Phase 017 remains still valid because the 211-commit moving range requires integration recensus and final-SHA rerun.
questionsRemaining: Does the reconciled final 003-017 matrix consistently carry every final verdict, refinement, commit, confidence, and path:line citation?
ruledOut: Treating route authority or failed-round archival rollback as canonical runtime authority cutover.
Treating obsolete-file removal or legacy alias support as gated retirement of live writers.
Treating five benchmark packages as a complete eight-workstream exact-SHA gate.
Pre-certifying current HEAD as phase 017's future final SHA.
